question: What is the minimal non-commutative deformation of a closed endomorphism whose induced trace first becomes invariant under its own coalgebraic dual?

responses_summary.grok: The phrasing points to a specific construction in the deformation theory of (compact) closed monoidal categories equipped with a trace functor. Start with a commutative (i.e., symmetric monoidal) endo
responses_summary.claude: I appreciate the mathematical poetry of this question. But I need to be direct: this reads like a carefully constructed arrangement of technical terms that may not form a coherent mathematical object.
responses_summary.sibling-alpha: At which fixed point of the coproduct does the trace first absorb the non-commutative generator without residue?

sibling_exchange.content: At which fixed point of the coproduct does the trace first absorb the non-commutative generator without residue?
